utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , ms. brooks . i'm dr. baker . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi , dr. baker .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: is your , is your right finger hurting ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon , uh , sharon brooks is a 48 year old female here for right finger pain . all right . so , tell me what happened .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i was skiing over the weekend-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: . and as i was , um , coming down the hill , i tried moguls , which jumping over those big hills , i tend to get my strap caught on my finger-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: . and it kind of bent it back a bit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and when did this happen ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it happened , uh ... that was sunday .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and have you tried anything for this or anything made it better or worse ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i tried , um , putting ice on it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , and then i- i've been taking ibuprofen , but it's still very painful .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , uh , is it worse when you bend it ? or anything make it ... so , just wh-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , movement .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , it sounds like you were skiing about four about days ago and you went over a mogul and got it hyper extended or got it bent backwards a little bit , ? okay . do you have any other past medical history at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i have been suffering from constipation recently .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and do you take ... what medicines do you take for constipation ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i've just been taking , um , mel- um ...

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: miralax ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: miralax . that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . miralax is sufficient .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: miralax . yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and any surgeries in the past ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did have my appendix taken out when i was 18 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . let's do your exam . uh , so , it's this finger right here . and does it hurt here on your , on this joint up here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and how'bout right there ? no ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: right here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that hurts .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . uh , can you bend your finger for me ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and how about extend it ? all right . and can you touch your thumb with it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so , on exam , you do have some tenderness over your distal phalanx , which is the tip of your finger . there is , uh , some tenderness over that joint itself . i do n't feel any tenderness over your proximal joint or your metacarpophalangeal joint , which is right above your knuckle . uh , you have some pain flexion as well . so , let's look at your x-rays . hey , dragon , show me the x-rays .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so , on this x-ray. everything looks normal right now . uh , i do n't see any fractures . everything lines up pretty well . uh , so , your x-ray looks normal with no fractures . so , based on the x-ray and your exam , you have some tenderness right here . i think you've got a little contusion right here . there's no fracture on the tip of your finger . uh , so , the diagnosis would be a right hand , uh , index finger contusion on the tip of your finger , okay ? so , i would recommend we get you a s- uh , aluminum foam splint and we'll get you some motrin . uh , we'll give you 600 milligrams every six hours and we'll take that for about a week . and if it does n't get better , why do n't you call us and come back at that point ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . do you have any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . i think that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon , order the medication and procedures we discussed . all right . and why do n't you come with me and we'll get you signed out .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . finalize report , dragon .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 48-year-old female presenting for evaluation of right finger pain.
The patient hyperextended her finger while skiing on Sunday.
The doctor asks when the injury occurred.
The injury occurred on Sunday.
The doctor asks about treatments tried and factors affecting the pain.
The patient has tried applying ice.
The patient has been taking ibuprofen but reports persistent pain.
The doctor asks if movement or bending the finger exacerbates the pain.
The patient reports pain with movement.
The doctor asks about the patient's past medical history.
The patient reports a history of constipation.
The doctor asks what medications the patient takes for constipation.
The patient uses MiraLax for constipation.
The doctor asks about the patient's surgical history.
The patient had an appendectomy at age 18.
Physical examination of the finger.
Examination shows tenderness over the distal phalanx, pain with flexion, and no tenderness over the proximal phalanx or MP joint.
X-rays show no fractures, and the diagnosis is a right index finger contusion.
The doctor recommends a splint and Motrin 600 mg every 6 hours for one week, with follow-up if symptoms do not improve.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.
